solution: 
```class Solution:
    def minimumLength(self, s: str) -> int:
        while len(s) > 1 and s[0] == s[-1]:
            prefix = 1
            while prefix < len(s) and s[0] == s[prefix]:
                prefix += 1
            suffix = 1
            while suffix < len(s) and s[-1] == s[-suffix - 1]:
                suffix += 1
            s = s[prefix:-suffix]
        return len(s)
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a while loop to continuously remove the prefix and suffix of the string as long as they are equal to the first and last characters of the string. The prefix and suffix are found using two while loops that traverse the string from the start and end respectively. The string is then sliced to remove the prefix and suffix. This process is repeated until the string can no longer be reduced. The time complexity is O(n) because each character in the string is visited at most twice, and the space complexity is O(n) because in the worst case, the string is reduced to a single character.
